compiled_state.title: Write failing integration tests for PR comment response
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: An integration test suite that exercises the complete PR comment response pipeline — fetch, classify, accept/defend/defer — against a mock GitHub API fixture. No real GitHub API calls escape during the test run.
**Why**: Establishes the RED-state baseline that all implementation stories must satisfy before any implementation begins. Proves the pipeline can execute end-to-end in a controlled environment.
**Scope**:
- IN: Mock GitHub API fixture serving a PR with exactly three comments: one accept, one defend, one defer (including a reply-in-thread comment to exercise root_comment_id chain-walk logic); mock boundary enforcement (assertion that no real gh api or MCP calls escape during test run); test assertions after pipeline runs: all three thread reply calls are verified, the accept-classified comment's fix commit is present in git log, the defer-classified comment's ticket ID is present in the normalized JSON, defense_comment_id is non-null for the defend-classified comment
- OUT: Real GitHub API interaction; exhaustive edge-case coverage (covered by unit tests in implementation stories); testing Story 5e (autonomy config) or Story 6 (merge integration) — those have their own tests

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a mock GitHub API fixture exists that serves a PR with one accept comment, one defend comment, and one defer comment including a reply-in-thread for root_comment_id chain-walk
  ← Satisfies: "In the integration test suite, a mock GitHub API fixture provides a PR with one accept, one defend, and one defer comment (including a reply-in-thread to exercise root_comment_id chain-walk)" (SC9)
- When this story is complete, the integration test suite can be run and confirmed FAILING (RED) before any implementation story begins; the failing run output is recorded as a ticket comment: .claude/scripts/dso ticket comment <this-story-id> "RED confirmed: <test output summary>"
  ← Satisfies: TDD RED-before-implementation requirement
- When this story is complete, the mock enforces that no real gh api calls or GitHub MCP tool calls escape during the test run (fails explicitly if the boundary is breached)
  ← Satisfies: "mock is scoped so no real gh api calls escape" (SC9)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified test scaffold logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement (test scaffold itself is tested)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
